title: AI and the Opportunity for Shared Prosperity: Lessons From the History of Technology and the Economy

reported_statistics.verbatim_quote: AI in one study creating significant value over existing analytical tools in 69% of business use-cases and emerging as the sole analytical solution in an additional 15%

a recent study that aggregated survey data across all U.S. occupations found that one-fourth of work tasks could be automated by AI, with particularly significant opportunities in administrative (46%) and legal (44%) professions

notes: This is a position/policy paper by Google executives and affiliated economists, not an original empirical research study. The paper synthesizes historical evidence about technology adoption and cites numerous case studies and research findings from other sources (McKinsey, Goldman Sachs, OECD, etc.) to argue that AI presents economic opportunity but requires deliberate policy action. The statistical results quoted are drawn from cited studies rather than original analysis by the authors. The paper focuses on economic opportunity and does not address safety, fairness, or other non-economic dimensions of AI in depth. No original experimental design, population, or arms are present. The paper includes numerous caveats that gains are not automatic, that benefits may not be evenly distributed, and that more research is needed as AI continues to develop.
